Mitsubishi Lancer EVO VIII MR GSR '04 - 4WD - 800PP - Racing Soft Tires - All Track - SWAP - 1.55
Feb 9, 2025
4 STARS CAR SETUP **** ENGINE SWAP: LS7-BRZ (Subaru BRZ Drift) Wide carRims "18 - Offset Wide - Rims wideFront B - Side B - Rear B - Wing Custom This car is extremely fast, clocking an impressive 2:05 on SPA, making it one of the top 3-4 road cars on this track. However, it’s not a car you can overdrive—late braking will punish you with significant time loss. If your driving inputs, especially braking, aren’t precise, you may find this car frustrating to handle. It demands accuracy and a steady hand to bring out its full potential. While it’s incredibly fast, it’s not a car suited for beginners. However, it’s an excellent tool for drivers looking to improve their skills and learn how to become faster and more precise on the track. Enjoy your drive. ><(((((((°>°°°°°°

600PPMRNordschleifeRenaultSWAP
Toyota MR2 GT-S '97 - 700PP - MR - Racing Hard Tires - All Track - SWAP - 1.55
Feb 7, 2025
4 STARS CAR SETUP **** Engine SWAP : BYH-R8 (Audi R8 4.2) -Wide body-Size "15 - Rims Width wide - Offset wide-Front B - Side B - Rear B - Wing Custom6:32 lap on Nordschleife—this is a top 700PP car, very close to earning a 5-star rating. Performant on any kind of track, the Audi R8 4.2 is fantastic: smooth, balanced, and powerful. You’ll never experience any unpleasant surprises when putting down the power. The balance is neutral for an MR car, but the front grip is superior, especially at high speeds, like when passing through the double left-hander “Pouhon.” It’s an amazing, grippy experience. Brakes are not so good , don't jump on 100% brakes , brake earlier with a 80% pressure and use trail brake technique , the car work a lot better this way.I’ll be working on a 600PP setup using the original engine very soon. You can also race at Le Mans with it using Fuel Mode 5 or 6, achieving competitive lap times of around 4 minutes. The excellent traction provided by this incredible engine will be a huge advantage, especially during wet laps on the track. Enjoy your drive. ><(((((((°>°°°°°°°
700PPNordschleifeSWAPToyota
Hyundai IONIQ 5 N '24 - 4WD - Sport Soft Tires - 700PP - All Track - 1.55
Feb 7, 2025
4 STARS CAR SETUP **** Wide car Rims "21 - Offset Wide - Rims wide -Front St - Side A - Rear A - Wing Standard I first tried setting this car up for the weekly race at 600PP using Sport Hard tires. You can win this way—the car is drivable and stable, but it’s boring to drive due to the nerfed power at 600PP. Afterward, I set up a maxed-out build using Sport Soft tires, reaching almost 700PP, and I have to say this car was a big surprise. It’s an excellent drive—fast, with tons of grip, very stable, and easy to race. It doesn’t even need racing tires. The idea is to use this setup in an online lobby with only two simple rules: maxed-out settings and Sport Tires only. It’s very easy to set up a room like this, and I’m sure it would lead to thrilling races. I love driving this car. The braking distance is a bit longer than other road cars, but as long as you adjust your braking points, it’s not an issue. Enjoy your drive. ><(((((((°>°°°°°°

5 STARS CAR SETUP *****New BOP 1.55 made this old carone of the best Gr3 car under BOP. This classic Nissan is a pleasure to drive, and this BOP setup works well with max power and a turbo. I plan to create a tutorial using this car, as a customized gearbox makes a significant difference with peaky engines. For cars with a well-spread powerband, almost any gearbox setup works with minimal lap time differences (as you’ll see using your ghost). But with peaky engines, a well-matched gearbox really stands out. I'm going to put a "Gr3 BOP" section in the "collections" close to "Home Page" button. 4 STARS CAR SETUP **** Golden oldies. 60s Le Mans cars. This new setup sits right between the two classic golden oldies: it offers slightly more grip than the Jaguar and is a bit faster than the Ferrari. On tracks like Spa or similar, their lap times are nearly identical. However, be cautious when driving over depressions, such as the exit of Eau Rouge or fast chicanes, as you can lose traction and spin. If this happens, modulate the throttle to regain control and avoid losing momentum. 4 legendary tracks. Daytona road / Le Mans / SPA and Nordschleife. All of them are reference all over the world since a long time. 550PPFRFerrari
Subaru Impreza Sedan WRX STI '04 - 4WD - 700PP - Racing Hard Tires - Nordschleife - SWAP 1.55
Feb 4, 2025
4 STARS CAR SETUP **** Engine SWAP: DKH-911 (Porsche 911 992 Turbo S '20) Wide Body Rims "18 - Rim width Wide Front A - Side B - Rear A - Wing B This is a top-tier road car on the Nordschleife at 700PP, capable of matching the pace of the Mitsubishi Lancer Evolution III and lapping 6:32 in my hands. However, this car can exhibit understeer from mid-corner to exit if not driven properly. To maximize its performance, it’s crucial to use trail braking technique when entering a turn. Additionally, avoid partial throttle input as you approach the apex—instead, wait until you’re on a better line before applying full throttle. The torque differential is set low at 35/65, which helps improve the car’s eagerness to rotate by reducing some front traction. This setup encourages a more responsive and agile driving experience. Enjoy your drive. ><(((((((°>°°°°°°

4 STARS CAR SETUP **** Wide Body Rims "15 - rims width wide - Offset wide Front A - Side St - Wing Custom This car is fast on any track, delivering consistent performance across a variety of circuits. It features a neutral balance, providing excellent grip and top speed. To get the most out of it, focus on staying on the optimal racing line and avoid overdriving the car. The car is incredibly stable and forgiving, making it easy to recover from mistakes or tricky situations. However, aggressive driving techniques—such as harsh braking, abrupt steering inputs, or excessive throttle—can compromise its performance. Instead, smooth and precise driving will yield the best lap times and overall results. Enjoy your drive. ><(((((((°>°°°°°°

Feb 3, 2025
5 STARS CAR SETUP ***** Wide Body Rims "20 - Rim width Wide - Offset Wide Front A - Side A - Rear Wing: Custom The setup is similar to the 800PP version, with just a few differences, such as softer springs. This is normal when transitioning from soft to hard compounds. The reduced weight transfer allows for smoother transitions. I use all the engine tuning options for this car to be able to set it up for both 800PP and 700PP on the same car. The grip is truly superior for a 700PP car, and the top speed is very good. The only slight drawback is slightly slower acceleration compared to other top 700PP cars, especially the 4WD models. Overall, the performance is excellent, lapping 6:31 on the Nordschleife while delivering an easy and pleasurable driving experience. Have a good drive. ><((((((°>°°°°°°°

700PPJaguarMR
Honda Civic Si Extra (EF) '87 - FF - 500PP - Sport Hard Tires - Nordschleife - SWAP - 1.55
Jan 31, 2025
4 STARS CAR SETUP **** Engine SWAP : B18C-Integra-'98 (Honda Integra Type R '98) Wide Body Size "15 Front B - Side B - Rear A - Wing A I in 4 STARS CAR SETUP **** Engine SWAP : B18C-Integra-'98 (Honda Integra Type R '98) Wide BodySize "15 Front B - Side B - Rear A - Wing A I initially tried setting up the car at 550PP, but the excessive wheel spin disrupted my driving line, which I found quite frustrating. On top of that, the performance just wasn’t where I wanted it to be. After some experimentation, I decided to drop down to the 500PP range. The lighter weight significantly improved grip, but I noticed the car was still lagging behind other top-tier 500PP front-wheel-drive (FF) cars in terms of raw performance. To address this, I added some ballast to free up room for more power, positioning it at +25 to help reduce understeer and improve the car’s agility. One of the things I love about this setup is how well the car rotates off-throttle, allowing me to aggressively attack apexes. It’s an incredibly fun and effective way to drive this Honda, and it feels rewarding when everything clicks. With these adjustments, the car is now on par with the Integra ’95 and other top-performing 500PP FF cars, which is a testament to how well it can perform when dialed in correctly. Enjoy your drive. ><(((((((°>°°°°°°

Gran Turismo F3500-A - MR - 950PP - Maxed - Racing Tires - All Track - 1.55
Jan 31, 2025
5 STARS CAR SETUP ***** Front A - Side St - Rear A I initially found the original car's setup to be poorly designed and unsuitable for players. However, after making adjustments, I transformed it into a highly competitive and enjoyable car. By balancing downforce levels (lowering front and rear equally for better top speed while maintaining high downforce for grip), I improved lap times on 90% of tracks. The car now excels in traction out of slow and medium corners and performs well on technical tracks, matching the pace of top Gr1 cars. I recommend avoiding high RPM shifts in the first three gears to maximize traction. The car is now steady, balanced, and a pleasure to drive. Enjoy your drive. ><(((((((°>°°°°°°

4 STARS CAR SETUP **** Rims size "17 - Width wide - Offset wide Front A - Wing A This is a top-tier 700PP car, capable of lapping 2:15 on SPA. However, it has several PP bugs. It’s very fast, grippy, and easy to drive, but there’s one situation where it can be tricky to control. Unfortunately, I couldn’t correct this behavior without compromising the car’s other strengths. 🚨 NEVER BRAKE TOO LATE. 🚨Late braking induces oversteer, causes rear grip loss, and slows the car down until it regains traction. The biggest issue comes when releasing the brake mid-corner. The best approach is to brake earlier and use trail braking. Be soft and smooth at this moment—you’ll be much faster this way. Not ok for Le Mans dur to huge gas consumption. Enjoy your drive. ><((((((((°>°°°°°°

4 stars700PPMRPorsche
1.55 Physics Simulation Model - Important changes - Brand new garage will be done.
Jan 30, 2025
1. Physics Simulation Model - The behavioral model for suspension systems has been adjusted.  ・Reduced sudden changes in behavior when driving over bumps or uneven surfaces.This is a great improvement. When going through strong compressions, like Fox Hole at Nordschleife, the car remains stable—you won’t experience for some cars a sudden weight balance shifts or losing the driving line. - The tire physics simulation model has been adjusted.  ・Adjustments have been made on load dependency while cornering.  ・Grip loss on racing lines on wet surfaces has been reduced.  ・The impact of dirty tires from off-track excursions has been reduced, with faster recovery of grip when returning to the track. This is exactly what I had been asking PD for a long time—less sudden loss of grip when exceeding the optimal traction, whether laterally or longitudinally under acceleration, braking, or cornering. Previously, grip would suddenly drop from 100% to 50%, but now it feels more like 100% to 75%. This results in a more progressive loss of grip, making the car easier to control, providing better feedback, and enhancing driving pleasure. Additionally, off-track surfaces—such as grass or the green zones beyond the curbs—now offer more grip. - Updates have been made to the handling of some cars.  ・Adjustments have been made to default aerodynamic settings for Gr.1 cars and some race cars.  ・Adjustments have been made to the default suspension and differential settings for some road cars. - Performance Points (PP) for some cars have been updated. - The effects of the following driving assists have been updated:  ・The Traction Control (TCS) assist intervention has been optimized.  ・Corner braking control for ABS (anti-lock brakes) has been optimized. It looks like they’ve fixed some broken cars… let’s see. The Garage RCR Civic seems more drivable now—the updated grip loss physics have significantly reduced front wheel spin, making it much more manageable.Most pre-1.54 PP setups need some adjustments. Traction control has been optimized, and the tire physics likely play a big role in this new behavior, along with the updated TC system. There’s now a better feel when entering corners under braking and improved feedback during trail braking.

You can push it hard—it’s a car that thrives on aggressive driving. It can feel epic at times, but it’s also easy to correct if you overdrive it. The driving line isn’t perfect, as the chassis struggles to fully manage the monster power, but it’s still very drivable and highly performant. Jan 25, 2025
4 STARS CAR SETUP **** Engine SWAP: LS7-BRZWide carRims "17 - Offset Wide - Rims wideFront A - Side B - Rear B - Wing Custom This swap engine has a terrible PP bug. It can support 1200+ HP, but I didn’t use a turbo for this setup to ensure smoother acceleration inputs, especially around the Tokyo wet track. Since I can’t use racing brakes, you’ll need to brake very early—both at the end of the straight and before the last turn. You can see the two braking points marked below. It’s not very fun to drive, but it’s drivable and extremely fast. You can lap 2:00 during a 25-minute race with one stop for gas and tires on lap 6. To complete the six laps, you’ll need to short shift or use FM mode. I also have a 700PP setup to publish very soon. Enjoy your drive. ><(((((((°>°°°°°° The braking point is just in front of the car shelter on the left.  For the second braking point, don’t downshift to 1st gear too early. Wait for the right RPM, or you’ll lose the mechanical engine braking effect.

Red Bull X2019 Comp - MR - 950PP - Racing Medium Tires - Interlagos Event - 1.54
Jan 20, 2025
4 STARS CAR SETUP **** The only difference with this setup is that there’s no PP limit for this event, unlike my previous 950PP setup. Because of this, I was able to lower the rear downforce to create a new balance with better turn-in by transferring more grip to the front. However, this also made the car more unstable and prone to spinning. To compensate, I lowered the rear ride height, reducing the rake to improve rear stability. As for the gearbox, it was an initial request by another member. I didn’t notice a significant difference compared to the stock gearbox, but this one is tuned to provide better safety and performance when exiting slow and medium-speed corners. You still need to be cautious when accelerating after the apex, especially when passing over kerbs. Using throttle control, proper driving inputs, or enabling traction control can help if you need more stability in those moments. Enjoy your drive. ><(((((((°>°°°°°°

4 STARS CAR SETUP **** Wide car Rims "18 - Offset Wide - Rims wide -Front B - Side A - Rear A - Wing Custom This is one of the fastest road cars on the Nordschleife, just 2 seconds behind the Ford Escort RS and Jaguar F-Type R '14. Abarth 595 SS '70 - RR - 460PP - Comfort Medium & Soft Tires - Alsace Mission - SWAP - 1.54
Dec 25, 2024
5 STARS CAR SETUP ***** Egine SWAP : K20C1-Civic-'20 (Honda Civic Type R '20)Wide bodyRims 14" - Rim Width Wide - Offset WideFront A - Wing A Exceptional performance for the Alsace Mission. With a mixed compound of tires (no need to change them during the race), the car is very well balanced and easy to handle. Just avoid overdriving it—maintain a consistent, fast pace, respect your braking points, and everything will go smoothly. Strategy: 9 laps per stint, with 2 stops for fuel only (no new tires required).I avoided weight reductions to keep the front end grounded, as the car can pop a wheelie when it’s too light. I didn’t use Engine Tuning parts to preserve fuel efficiency. There’s room to install them, but they don’t significantly improve lap times and will increase fuel consumption—important for a 9-lap stint. To manage fuel, you’ll need to short-shift throughout the race. If you choose to install Engine Tuning parts, you can use all the available options except for the High Compression Pistons. It’s up to you. This setup makes it easy to complete a 26-lap race, putting you near the top of the rankings. A very skilled driver might even reach 27 laps. Enjoy your drive. ><(((((((°>°°°°°°

I initially tried setting this car to 600PP, but the wheel spin out of slow and medium-speed corners was too annoying. The gearbox is tuned for the Nordschleife, but the car can perform well on other tracks. Just adjust the gears to be shorter using the final gear setting. Have a wonderful Christmas evening! 800PPAMGFRMercedes
Mitsubishi Lancer Evo Final Gr4 - 4WD - BOP - Racing Tires - All Track - 1.54
Dec 5, 2024
This is a very fast Gr4 car with excellent top speed, and I’ve fine-tuned a sharp, high-performance build. The car handles beautifully under trail braking, but wheel input plays a crucial role in maintaining control. It requires a light and precise touch, especially in specific conditions—like high-speed braking during a turn. For example, at Trial Mountain, entering the first tunnel or braking hard at the end of the straight after the third tunnel demands careful steering. If you apply too much wheel angle, you risk losing the rear end. In other scenarios, however, the car remains steady and easy to handle. I chose not to completely eliminate this behavior, as doing so would compromise the car’s overall performance and other excellent handling characteristics. Enjoy your drive . ><(((((((°>°°°°°° __________________________________________________________________ I'm going to put a "Gr4 BOP" section in the "collections" close to "Home Page" button. They'll race under BOP rules , only weight and power , but with full open settings. BOP is the only way to have a similar performance for all Gr3 cars race fairly. Suspension , Differential , Downforce , Gearbox , brake balance only will be tunable. You can adapt your car for a specific track setting your transmission and downforce only. Downforce setting need to be setted same value front and rear to keep the good balance. Suspension , Differential are ok for any kind of track.

550PPFFHondaSWAP
How to apply a Praiano tune
Nov 12, 2024
https://www.gt-setups.cc/How to apply a Praiano tune. First of all , check update your app the latest update.1. Check if you own the car. Check to ensure its the correct model and year. Some cars have similarly named models. Also be aware that there might be a "Road Car" variant of a race car.2. If you don't already own it, buy the car. Select it as current vehicle.3. Navigate to "GT Auto" and check whether your car has wide body and compare to Praiano instructions at top of text for the tune. If Praiano tune is normal body and you have already installed wide body, then you won't be able to match the tune exactly. You need to buy the car again, because a wide body modification cannot be reversed. The same applies to a swapped engine. If Praiano tune uses the stock engine and you have already installed a swap engine in the car, then you cannot reverse this. You need to buy the car again.4. Navigate to Garage > Car Settings and check the current settings and parts installed on your car and compare it to the Praiano tune. Pay specific attention to the engine and body mods at the bottom right of screenshot 4 of the tune that say "installed". If your car already says "installed" for these engine parts (such as "polished ports") or body changes (weight reductions and/or rigidity) and they are not installed on the Praiano tune, then you'll need to purchase a new body and/or engine or buy the car again to get it back to stock. Those installed parts cannot be removed. Check in the "Extreme" tab of the "Tuning Shop" to see whether its more cost effective to buy a new car, or replace the engine/body.5. If Praiano's setting requires a wide body, go to "GT Auto" and install wide body.6. If Praiano's setting requires swap engine, go to "GT Auto" and install swap engine (or find it in "Tuning Parts" in the Garage, if you already own the engine from a wheel spin prize)7. [Optional] Navigate to Customization > Content Library and find a livery that you like. Download it and apply it to the car.8. In Customization Parts, match the Front, Side, Rear and Wing settings to the Praiano tune description.9. Match the Rims to Praiano's tune description, paying attention to Rim Width and Offset which could be standard or wide.[Note: the order of steps 7, 8 and 9 matters. Every time you enter the Customization parts menu or download a new livery, the rims will reset to standard so you need to be sure to re-do steps (8) and (9) after changing any customization parts, or new livery download.10. Exit GT Auto and navigate to Garage > Car Settings and look at the tuning sheet. Look at the permanent body parts and engine tuning parts and compare to Praiano's to see what you need to buy from the "Tuning Parts" shop.11. Go to "Tuning Parts" and buy all tyres, tuning parts and body weight reductions including rigidity that are shown in the Praiano tune.Take special care to install the correct gearbox (usually Racing, but sometimes Manual)12. Go back to Garage > Car Settings and open the settings sheet. Match all the numbers to Praiano's settings sheet.13. Check that the PP matches Praiano's setting's PP. If it doesn't, check the HP to see if it matches. If they don't match, you've missed something or done something slightly wrong. Pay attention to detail. Check the permanent parts at bottom right of sheet 4. Sometimes the discrepancy will be because you need the a High Lift Camshaft/Bore Up/Stroke up S part that is found in the "Ultimate" tab in the Tuning Shop.14. Gearbox: To do the gearbox, set top speed to the number shown on Praiano's sheet, then go into the "Manual adjustment" section and set the final gear number, and then the individual ratios from bottom to top. Check that the speeds and ratios match Praiano's. Sometimes it will seem like the ratios won't reach high enough numbers, but due to bugs in the game, they will open up. For example if you're setting 6th gear and it say the number should be 0.851, and the highest you can get is 0.600, then move on to doing 5th gear and then 4th gear and 3rd gear. Then reopen 6th gear and you'll see that you can move the slider further right and reach higher numbers than before. Don't ask why. Its a quirk of the game.15. Do a final PP check. If all numbers match then you're done.Sometimes GT7 updates change PP calculations and the numbers will not match if the tune was done on an earlier GT7 version (such as 1.52). If this is the case and you need the number to fit just below a certain point like 700PP, just use the ECU or power restrictor to raise or lower the PP by the small amount necessary. For Android (Google Play Store) The app was last notably updated around January 5, 2026, with bug fixes and features like improved fan profiles. Open the Google Play Store app on your Android device.Tap the search bar at the top and type "Patreon".Select the official Patreon app (developed by Patreon, with millions of downloads and high rating).If an update is available, you'll see an Update button — tap it to download and install the latest version.If it says "Open" instead, you're already up to date! Alternatively, if you have auto-updates enabled, it may update automatically. For troubleshooting issues, some users recommend uninstalling/reinstalling to force the latest version. Tip: Many recent user reports mention the app getting occasional bugs (like UI changes or video playback), so updating often resolves them. If problems persist, try the mobile website in a browser as a workaround. For iOS (iPhone/iPad - App Store) The iOS version recently received updates (e.g., version 125.38.0 with performance fixes, as of early January 2026). Open the App Store on your iOS device.Tap the Search icon (magnifying glass) at the bottom.Search for "Patreon".Select the official Patreon app (by Patreon).If an update is available, tap Update. (If it shows "Open", you're current!)Authenticate with Face ID, Touch ID, or your Apple ID password if prompted. Important note for iOS users: New memberships/subscriptions made directly in the app may include Apple's 30% fee (which goes to Apple, not creators). To avoid this and get the best deal for creators, many recommend signing up/upgrading via a web browser (patreon.com) on any device instead. Once subscribed, the app works great for browsing/content. Quick tip: Always keep your phone's OS updated too, as app compatibility can depend on it. The Patreon app is available for free on both platforms — download from official stores here if needed: iOS: Search in App Store or visit patreon.com/mobileAndroid: Search in Google Play or visit patreon.com/mobile If you're running into specific issues (e.g., crashing, loading problems), let me know your device/OS version for more tailored advice! While all Gr4 cars are within half a second per lap, the 4WD options tend to be easier to push hard.Enjoy your drive. ><(((((((°>°°°°°° I'm going to put a "Gr4 BOP" section in the "collections" close to "Home Page" button. They'll race under BOP rules , only weight and power , but with full open settings. BOP is the only way to have a similar performance for all Gr3 cars race fairly. Suspension , Differential , Downforce , Gearbox , brake balance only will be tunable. You can adapt your car for a specific track setting your transmission and downforce only. Downforce setting need to be setted same value front and rear to keep the good balance. Suspension , Differential are ok for any kind of track.

4 STARS CAR SETUP **** -Wide body -Standard Wheels-Size "15 - Offset wide -Front A - Wing St I spent 4 hours trying to set this "No wingversion" car at 600 or 700PP, but couldn’t find a way to stabilize the rear end, which feels like it has a mind of its own. The gearbox is tuned for Nordschleife, but you can lower the final gear setting for slower tracks. Keep in mind that max power is at 5100 RPM, dropping sharply beyond that. Aim to reach 5100 RPM at the fastest point of the track for optimal performance. Enjoy your drive.><((((((((°>°°°°°°

Ford Mustang Mach 1 '71 - FR - 700PP - Racing Medium Tires - All Track - SWAP - 1.52
Oct 12, 2024
4 STARS CAR SETUP ***** Engine SWAP: Coyote-5.0L-Mustang (Ford Mustang GT '15) Wide body car. Rims size 16" - Rim Width Wide - Offset Wide Front A - Wing Custom The main challenge with this car was managing the huge torque from the Coyote 5.0 engine. To address this, I started by adding maximum rear ballast and using softer medium tires to load the rear tires and improve traction. Once that issue was resolved, I found a new balance with a 50/50 weight distribution. I spent 2 hours testing several options against my best ghost on multiple tracks, and I’m very happy with the result. It’s not perfect, but the setup does a great job of containing this crazy engine. Enjoy your drive. ><(((((((°>°°°°°°

4 STARS CAR SETUP **** Engine SWAP: Evo-Final-Gr.B (Mitsubishi Lancer Evolution Gr.B Rally Car) Wide car Rims "16 - Offset Wide - Rims wide -Front B - Side A - Rear A - Wing Custom This setup could be rated 5 stars in terms of performance, with lap times of 2:17 on SPA, 6:30 on Nordschleife, and 3:50 on Le Mans using Racing Hard tires for impressive top speed. It’s an extremely high-performance car, no doubt. However, the handling is just good, not exceptional, and far from perfect. To improve grip, I use Racing Medium tires, and while the top speed remains very good—faster than a Porsche 911 '16. This car was broken before update 1.52, but this setup works well for both the EVO V and EVO VI. Enjoy your drive. ><(((((((°>°°°°°°

Renault Trophy '11 - Gr4 - MR - BOP - Racing Tires - All Track - 1.52
Oct 4, 2024
This Renault offers superior grip and good top speed. I’ve set it up with high front and low rear downforce to favor turn-in at any speed. While this kind of setup is usually aggressive and difficult to handle, I balanced it with softer rear springs and a higher deceleration differential. This allows the car to deliver both excellent cornering and stability. The result is a very fast yet safe car, offering the best of both worlds. You can find  "Gr4 BOP" section in the "collections" close to "Home Page" button. They'll race under BOP rules , only weight and power , but with full open settings. BOP is the only way to have a similar performance for all Gr4 or Gr3 cars race fairly. Suspension , Differential , Downforce , Gearbox , brake balance only will be tunable. You can adapt your car for a specific track setting your transmission and downforce only. Downforce setting need to be setted same value front and rear to keep the good balance. Suspension , Differential are ok for any kind of track. Enjoy your drive. ><(((((((°>°°°°°°

Volvo 240 SE Estate '93 - FR - 500PP - Sport Hard Tires - Nordschleife - 1.66
Oct 2, 2024
4 STARS CAR SETUP **** Wide Car Rims "16 Rim Width Wide - Offset wide Front B - Side B - Rear B - Wing A This car always surprises me in the best way. Back in the early '80s, when I was a windsurfer, this was my dream car—perfect for loading my windsurf gear and crashing on the beach after a night out. But it was out of my budget, so I settled for a cheaper Opel Rekord 2.0 wagon, which was also a great car.In the game, after maxing out all the parts, this car drives like a Supercar MR with excellent mid-engine handling characteristics. It’s very fun to drive and performs impressively on the Nordschleife. Power is only available at very high RPMs. With superior grip, this car could race against the best 500 PP cars, but the top speed is just good—not very fast—perhaps due to the square front? Enjoy your drive. ><(((((((°>°°°°°°

Lexus RC F GT3 '17 - FR - 800PP - Racing Soft Tires - All Track - 1.50
Sep 30, 2024
The BOP settings are working exceptionally well with this maxed setup. I’ve increased a few spring rates, and the gearbox is specifically tuned for Daytona. While this car doesn't match the top speed and acceleration of other maxed Gr3 cars like the Ford GT or McLaren F1 (which are about 200 lbs lighter with similar power), it compensates with superior grip. It offers a very steady driving line and is easy to handle. The only potential issue is some rear wheel spin out of slow corners, so I recommend using TC 1 to mitigate that if necessary. Enjoy your drive. ><(((((((°>°°°°°°

950PPMRSuper Formula
One broken Porsche 911 '92 and a nerfed Gr1 Bugatti
Sep 26, 2024
I feel sorry for those who requested these two cars, but I won’t be publishing them. For the Bugatti Gr1, pre-1.49, it was a top-tier Gr1 car, capable of matching the best lap times. Unfortunately, after the update, it’s totally nerfed and about 5 seconds slower than the top cars at SPA or Sardegna, whether at 800PP, 900PP, or even 950PP when maxed out. As for the Porsche 911 '92, it’s the worst 911 in the game. It has unpredictable, strange behaviors that I couldn’t fix, no matter how extreme the settings. It’s just a medium-performance 700PP car with no driving pleasure. Luckily, there are plenty of other 911 gems in the game to enjoy. Enjoy your drive using others cars. Sorry . ><(((((((°>°°°°°°°
Dodge Charger SRT Hellcat '15 - FR - 700PP - Racing Hard Tires - All Track - 1.50
Sep 26, 2024
4 STARS CAR SETUP **** -Wide body-Size "20 / Rims width wide / Offset wide-Front A- Side A - Rear St - Wing Custom With such high power and substantial torque at the rear wheels, this car initially suffers from uncontrollable wheel spin, making it challenging to manage and leading to significant traction loss. To correct this behavior, I adjusted the original front-rear balance from 54/46 to 50/50. This change restored traction and eliminated the need to constantly monitor for loss of grip, resulting in a much more manageable driving experience. Adjusting the front-rear balance to 50/50 not only restored traction but also added front grip, resulting in a steady and precise driving line. It’s transformed the driving experience into a real pleasure, making the car much more enjoyable and manageable on the road. Enjoy your drive. ><(((((((°>°°°°°°
700PPDodgeFR

Community Interest List Now Available!
Sep 25, 2024
We are excited to announce that the community interest list is now being shared. If the car you previously requested hasn’t been published yet, it might have been missed. We sincerely apologize for this and kindly ask that you send your request again. Please send us a private message here on Patreon, and we will be happy to add your desired car to the list for future publications. Thank you for your support and understanding. We look forward to hearing from you! # )
Audi TTS Coupé '14 - 4WD - 700PP - Racing Hard Tires - Nordschleife - 1.50
Sep 21, 2024
4 STARS CAR SETUP **** Wide car Rims "19 - Offset Wide - Rims wide -Front A - Side A - Wing Custom I took a different approach for this setup—no ballast, with some rake, and still a strong differential setting to keep the car stable through the four suspensions. Without this tighter differential, the car feels too free, almost uncontrollable, especially on the Nordschleife, where it becomes a real trap. This setup performs just as well as the SWAP version, though perhaps a couple of seconds slower, lapping around 6:32. It’s still very pleasant to drive, with no front-end laziness. I’ve adjusted the differential distribution to 35/65, which is well-balanced and gives the car a nice eagerness out corners. Enjoy your drive. ><(((((((°>°°°°°°
4 stars4WD700PPAUDINordschleife
Audi TTS Coupé '14 - 4WD - 600PP - Sport medium Tires - Nordschleife - 1.50
Sep 19, 2024
4 STARS CAR SETUP **** Wide car Rims "19 - Offset Wide - Rims wide -Front A - Side A - Wing Custom The original setup of this car shows significant understeer, so I’ve made a few key adjustments. By adding rear ballast and increasing the rake, I’ve improved the car’s balance. The suspension is also tuned to enhance front grip during trail braking, allowing for better apex control at any speed. The key is to stay light on the pedal and precise at the wheel. With this setup, it becomes an easy and fun driving experience, where reaching the apex feels smooth and natural. Enjoy your drive. ><(((((((°>°°°°°°

Sep 15, 2024
4 STARS CAR SETUP **** Wide Car. Rims 17" - rims width wide - Offset wide Front A - Side St - Wing B This is a mid-engine, rear-wheel drive (MR) car, and it behaves exactly as you’d expect from such a setup. It requires a bit of technical finesse, especially since it doesn’t offer much grip on harder compound tires. Precision is key here, but the car handles predictably and reliably—it’s stable, with no major issues. I prefer driving in cockpit view, which feels particularly immersive. The wide, clean field of vision enhances performance, making it easier to hit those perfect lines. While the top speed isn't the car’s strongest suit, the real strength lies in its handling. In the corners, it’s incredibly responsive, providing superior control through the bends. It’s a car that rewards skill, and I absolutely love driving it. Enjoy your drive. ><(((((((°>°°°°°°
550PPMRNordschleifeRenault
Nissan Fairlady Z 300ZX TT 2S '89 - FR - 700PP - Racing Hard Tires - Nordschleife - 1.50
Sep 14, 2024
5 STARS CAR SETUP ***** Wide Body Rims "17 - Rim width Wide - Offset Wide Front B - Side B - Rear B - Wing custom This is a real surprise after physic update , it was a good car before but now it's almost a perfect car in my opinion. Perfect driving line and reactive to all inputs , staying easy for any level of drivers. With a superior grip and excelent top speed , performance as acceleration are just good , same as others top 700PP cars. It's a real pleasure to follow the fast driving line without problems , just the way you've anticipated. On Nordschleife it's 6:30 and on Le Mans using Fuel mode 6 it's 4:04. Not so good but with a real driving pleasure , same on any others tracks. It's a car you'll use a lot for any other event. Enjoy your drive. ><(((((((°>°°°°°° Edit: i did a test on the SWAP engine setted at 800PP , max power and Racing Soft , everything maxed , but the car don't support all this power . It's not a good option at all. You can the same setup 700PP with it , same behaviors and performance as original engine.
5 STARS700PPFRNissanNordschleife
TVR Tuscan Peed 6 '00 - FR - 700PP - Racinh Hard Tires - All Track - 1.50
Sep 13, 2024
4 STARS CAR SETUP **** Wide Car. Rims 17" - rims width wide - Offset wide Front A - Side A - Rear A - Wing Custom Same behaviors as the SWAP engine The SWAP have a few more power and torque for same weight , around 15 HP more . It's a top 700PP car with a pace similar to the Porsche 911 GT3 RS (991) '16. It’s stable, easy to drive, and has excellent top speed and grip. The neutral balance makes it perfect for technical tracks like the Nordschleife, Spa, or similar circuits. Have a good drive. ><(((((((°>°°°°°°

Toyota Celica GT-FOUR (ST205) '94 - 4WD - 600PP - Sport Medium Tires - All Track - 1.50
Sep 10, 2024
4 STARS CAR SETUP **** -Wide body-Size "18 - Rims Width wide - Offset wide-Front B - Side B - Rear A - Wing A Good car, easy to drive, with a similar pace to the Mazda 3, just slightly faster, around 7:05 on the Nordschleife. I added 200 kg of ballast because the lighter version was too slow, lacking the horsepower to reach the same 600PP. Because of this, you don't need to buy all the weight reduction stages—just the one needed to match the weight. Enjoy your drive. ><(((((((°>°°°°°°

4 STARS CAR SETUP **** Wide Body. Rims "14 & "15 - Rim width Wide - Offset Wide Front A - Wing A I remember well when I was young in the '80s, watching Jean Ragnotti on TV, driving this car like a madman around the Rally Tour de Corse. Toyota Celica GT-FOUR (ST205) '94 - 4WD - 600PP - Sport Hard Tires - Tokyo - 1.50
Sep 7, 2024
4 STARS CAR SETUP **** -Wide body-Size "18 - Rims Width wide - Offset wide-Front B - Side B - Rear A - Wing A Excelent car for Tokyo rain event. Decent top speed around 306 kmh = 190 mph Very good grip , a pleasure through the bends. Superior brakes , you can brake later than anyother car safely without missing apex. Perfect for beginners to learn the track making some $$$. Laping 2:09 / 2:08 in my hands regular , 1 stop for gas only, normally tires can do 12 laps. Up to you. Enjoy your drive. ><(((((((°>°°°°°°°
4WD600PPTokyoToyota
Toyota Celica GT-FOUR (ST205) '94 - 4WD - 700PP - Racing Hard Tires - All Track - 1.50
Sep 7, 2024
4 STARS CAR SETUP **** -Wide body-Size "16 - Rims Width wide - Offset wide-Front B - Side B - Rear B - Wing Custom This is the All Track setup , having a good rotation, good reactivity to inputs. Perfect for SPA or any technical circuit with a good flow and fluids lines. This setup don't work for Nordschleife. A lot of unpredictable moves under brakes during weight transfer , also wild over kerbs. I had to tight it around the 4 corners. There is an easy solution for this : SET THE DIFFERENTIAL THIS WAY FOR NORDSCHLEIFE ONLY Front 40/20/50 Rear 40/30/50 Set also the gearbox using final gear only to : 5th gear 335 kmh = 208 mph The car is 6:31 on Nordschleife in my hands and 2:17.5xx on SPA , together with the top 700PP cars. Enjoy your drive. ><(((((((°>°°°°°°°
4WD700PPNordschleifeToyota
Subaru BRZ GT300 '21 - FR - BOP - Racing Tires - All Track - 1.50
Sep 6, 2024
For this car i've setted the downforce to minimum tofavor speed. The grip level is still very good . Set it same amount front and rear the way you want if need more grip for very turny track for example. I'm going to put a "Gr3 BOP" section in the "collections" close to "Home Page" button. They'll race under BOP rules , only weight and power, but with full open settings. BOP is the only way to have a similar performance for all Gr3 cars race fairly. Suspension, Differential, Downforce , Gearbox, brake balance only will be tunable. You can adapt your car for a specific track setting your transmission and downforce only. Downforce setting need to be setted same value front and rear to keep the good balance. Suspension, Differential are ok for any kind of track. Enjoy your drive. ><((((((°>°°°°°°°°

4 AM Brasil / 7 AM GMT / 3 AM EDT / 0 AM PDT // September 1 - 2024
Aug 31, 2024
4 Golden Oldies release tomorrow morning
Aug 30, 2024
4 AM Brasil / 7 AM GMT / 3 AM EDT / 0 AM PDT // August 31 - 2024 Golden oldies. 60s Le Mans cars. 😁👌👍 4 legendary tracks. Daytona road / Le Mans / SPA and Nordschleife. All of them are reference all over the world since a long time. All are perfectly balanced, easy to drive, for any player level, from beginner or alien, all very performants, a real thrill to drive. They are staying in the collections wall by brand of course and in a special "Golden Oldies" section. Old legendaries race cars, perfect to race together, the Praiano's BOP Special was setted by myself of course, after a lot of hours on severals legendaries tracks, car by car, comparing with all the ghost, test after test, to match perfectly all car's performances. It was a very hard work during the whole week beside all the others cars i did during this week. Enjoy your drive and have fun. ><(((((((°>°°°°°°

Honda Civic Type R (EK) TC - FF - 600PP - Sport Soft Tires - Tokyo rain - SWAP - 1.50
Aug 27, 2024
4 STARS CAR SETUP **** ENGINE SWAP: K24A-Civic - Size "17 - Rims width wide - Offset wide -Front B - Wing B Normally i don't like to do those powerfull FF cars due to the annoying wheel spin. This was a request and i try my best to produce the best i could do focusing to handling first and i think i reach my first goal. Fortunately Tokyo is a very fast flowing track with just a few acceleration out slow corners zone, where wheel spin is present. (I don't advise this car for Tsukuba or anything like this.) You have to do 6 laps on Sport soft tires , if you spin like a crazy , you'll not do the 6 laps. So take care , modulate your throttle , use anti-lag sdtrong mode , it help, or use traction control. I don't like the TC. It also seems that on this car , you can spin front wheel till 5th gear on dry tarmac , even on TC5. Crazy are the FF in GT7 or any GT in the history. This car lap at 2:00 out of traffic , you can't sustain this pace due to tire wear/wheel spin, but you can lap 2:02 taking care of the rubber. Enjoy your drive. ><(((((((°>°°°°°°°
600PPFFHondaSWAPTokyo
Mitsubishi GTO Twin Turbo '91 - 4WD - 700PP - Racing Hard Tires - All Track - SWAP - 1.52
Aug 25, 2024
4 STARS CAR SETUP **** ENGINE SWAP: H25A-Escudo -Wide body - Size "15 - Rims width wide - Offset wide -Front A - Side A - Rear A - Wing Custom Very good car , just would need a perfect handling to earn 5 stars but the performances are here. Driving pleasure is just good not perfect. 3:52 on Le Mans event 1 lap, starting FM 1 and 2 beginning the straight. 2:16 high on SPA 6:30 on Nordschleife with a stuning top speed at last straight.Easy car, steady, extremely fast. Just don't perform late hard braking entering a corner. It's not dangerou , you'll slidw and lose time that's all. Use trail braking instead, it work very well. Enjoy your drive your drive. ><((((((((°>°°°°°°°

Honda NSX GT500 '08 - Gr2 - MR - 900PP (Maxed) - Racing Soft Tires - All Track - 1.50
Aug 24, 2024
5 STARS CAR SETUP ***** For people to like to race Gr2 cars with a maxed setup. It's not performant against Gr1 at 900PP . It's an amazing car , ton of grip , perfect driving line , sane and safe. Any kind of fast driver, beginner or pro. Enjoy your drive. ><(((((((°>°°°°°°°°
Mitsubishi GTO Twin Turbo '91 - 4WD - 600PP - Sport Hard Tires - Tokyo Rain event - SWAP - 1.50
Aug 24, 2024
4 STARS CAR SETUP **** ENGINE SWAP: H25A-Escudo -Wide body- Size "15 - Rims width wide - Offset wide-Front A - Side A - Rear A - Wing Custom Normally, people favor a lot more power and top speed for this kind of dominants cars to race Tokyo Rainy event. I don't do likke this 100% . Only partially. Those cars are bad during curvy S side of the circuit. Showing a lot of understeer, you have to wait the car turn patiently, staying off all your driving inputs.... I don't have patience or any fun doing this . It's a 26 minutes race to make for credits$. If you don't have fun doing this, it's a pain not a pleasure. So i use a few rear ballast and enough front downforce, same performance as the others "Top speed builds". The car is not a driving machine as some few fantastic cars in this class i have in my garage but it's performant and fun. It's 25:50 in my hands. You need a compromise to race Tokyo. No speed, no performance. No grip, no fun... Enjoy your drive. ><((((((((°>°°°°°°°°°

Aug 23, 2024

Porsche 959 '87 - 4WD - 600PP - Sport Medium Tires - Nordschleife - 1.50
Aug 23, 2024
4 STARS CAR SETUP **** Rims 17" Front A - Wing A This car could be a wonderfull 700PP , it can carry a lot of power on racing tires but the original build is totally broken by Gran Turismo conceptors. It seems that so much weight transfer over racing tires is too much for the suspensions limits. No way to contain those bad behaviors even using extreme settings . A lot of cars are in this case after physics update. By chance we can use those cars in a lower power x grip configuration. This mytical Porsche is very good to drive on Nordschleife and very close from the top 600PP cars. It's safe fast and fun. I just tried to save this wonderful car in game. Mission accomplished. Enjoy your drive. ><(((((((°>°°°°°°°

Chevrolet Corvette (C2) '63 - FR - 600PP - Sport Medium Tires - All track - 1.50
Aug 21, 2024
4 STARS CAR SETUP **** Wide Car. Rims 17" - rims width wide - Offset wide Front B - Rear A - Wing Custom One of the most beautiful cars from '60 era in my opinion. Too bad that GT7 developers almost kill it. The car is undrivable when stock. Horrible. I think i did one of the best save since a long time. It's very good now, performant, not a very top 600PP but very fast and a pleasure to drive, showing precision and responsive to any inputs. At 700PP it's not ok, to much for those suspensions even on "Stiff" mode. No problem. It's a very good car and very nice to drive cockpit view or VR2. A lot of brown leather top quality. People was building this marvel for $4,037 base price in 1963 ..... when i'm born.... Enjoy your drive. ><(((((((°>°°°°°°°

New Search Tool
Aug 21, 2024
Introducing the new search tool created by a subscriber to help and streamline the process of finding your favorite car in Praiano63's Patreon garage. This tool is designed to make it easier and more efficient for you to locate the desired car among the collection. With this search tool, you can save time and quickly find the car you are looking for. Say hello to a more convenient car browsing experience where the idea is to search for the exact model you are looking for.
Lamborghini Countach 25th Anniversary '88 - MR - 600PP - Sport Medium Tires - All Track - 1.50
Aug 20, 2024
4 STARS CAR SETUP **** Wide Car. Rims 17" - rims width wide - Offset wide Front A - Side St - Wing Custom I've spent all my afternoon trying to set this car , the weight balance window is very narrow , difficult to find the sweet spot , combined with downforce balance. After this i could adapt this base for 3 kind of setups for this single car. Sport Hard / 600PP / for Tokyo rain event . (Very performant there on Tokyo) Sport Medium / 600PP / Grip oriented all track (medium performance but very pleasant to drive) Racing Hard / 700PP / All track , with a very good top speed (ok for Le Mans) Sound engine is very good for cockpit view or VR2. THIS SETUP IS OK FOR SWAP ENGINE : L539-Aventador-SV     ok for 700PP or + with softers compounds. Enjor your drive. ><(((((((°>°°°°°°°

1.49 to 1.50 update.
Aug 7, 2024
So the 1.50 update is there , without correcting the littles physics things i thought they would fix. They only correct the weird events when some cars was jumping very high , like stepping over a mine. The most important was to correct the sudden lost of rear end grip on certain cars , i keep try several time but there is no way using any settings availables to correct this bad behaviors. I could able to limit it but for some cars , they are almost impossible to drive with a kind of pleasure. They will sleep taking dust at the back of my garage unfortunately. The new Impreza '98 , or Audi TTS '09.... and some others are in this case. I'll try to save what could be saved. Sorry for this. Praiano63 . Laurent.
Toyota Corolla Levin 1600GT APEX (AE86) '83 - FR - 700PP - Racing Soft Tires - All Track - SWAP - 1.49
Aug 6, 2024
5 STARS CAR SETUP ***** Engine swap: 3S-GTE-MR2 (Toyota MR2 '97) Wide car - Rims size 16" - Rim Width Wide - Offset Wide Front Standard - Rear A - Wing Custom Fantastic iconic little car, very fun and rewarding drive. Very performant, steady, can fight with any top 700PP beast. I didn't test this setup with the Trueno model, but normally as far as i remember, they are the same original build in GT7 at least. Enjoy your drive. ><(((((°>°°°°°°°

Chevrolet Corvette ZR1 (C4) '89 - FR - 700PP - Racing Hard Tires - All track - 1.49
Aug 4, 2024
4 STARS CAR SETUP **** Wide CarRims 19"Rims With Wide - Offset WideFront A - Side A - Rear B - Wing Custom Even with the rear diffuser adding extra grip , there is still room some a good amount of HP for 700PP and the result a very fast very grippy car. A few rear ballast give some more eagerness , easy to place to apex , very stable but also reactive. Top 700PP performance car together with a most of the new 1.49 field 700PP. Perfect to race online together using differents cars with the promise of hard fair battles. Great programme. Have a good drive. ><(((((°>°°°°°°°

Subaru Impreza 22B STI '98 - 4WD - 700 PP - Racing medium Tires - All Tracks - 1.49
Aug 2, 2024
Wide bodyRims 17" / Rim width Wide / Offset WideFront part ASide ARear Wing : Custom . The Bolide ! Think about a very fast grippy little car you can drive very hard and very reactive. This is it. 22B legend. Top top 700PP car . From Nordschleife by Tsukuba to SPA , a few understeer as most 4WD yes but you can control all this and minimize it using all your drivings inputs. Sure you'll not fall asleep with this Subaru icon.
Nissan Fairlady 240ZG (HS30) '71 - FR - 600PP - Sport Medium Tires - All Track - 1.49
Jul 31, 2024
Wide carRims "14Width wideOfffset WideFront A - Wing CustomTop 3 600PP on Nordschleife from the news 1.49 update cars , same pace as Lambo Miura. A bit technical to drive but nothing hairy , just need precision in your wheel inputs. I've spent around 4 hours looking for the safer and faster option.You can use the same setting for Tokyo , just set Sport hard tires , front downforce at 32 , power restrictor at 92 and the top speed at 6th gear to 345 kmh = 214 mph.
